going_concern.language: In our judgment, there are conditions or events, considered in the aggregate, that raise substantial doubt about Boston Pizza Restaurants, LP's ability to continue as a going concern for a reasonable period of time.

notes.title: Description of Business
notes.content: Boston Pizza Restaurants, LP is a Delaware limited partnership (the Partnership or Company) that operates as the franchisor of Boston's The Gourmet Pizza (the business). The Partnership's business activities relate to the development of Boston's The Gourmet Pizza ® system and the offer, sale, and support of the franchised Business. There were 24, 22, and 25 franchise-owned restaurants at December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022, respectively. There was no restaurant location owned and operated by related parties in 2024 and one in 2023 and 2022.

key_facts.limited_partner: Treliving Private Investments Ltd. (TPI) — Canadian entity with 99% ownership position
key_facts.general_partner: Boston Pizza Restaurants GP, Inc. (BPRGP) — US entity

sub_notes.detail: Prepared in accordance with U.S. GAAP. Includes all adjustments consisting of normal recurring accruals. References to ASC and ASU issued. FASB as authoritative GAAP source.
Certain financial statement reclassifications made to prior-year amounts for current-year presentation. No impact on statements of operations previously reported.
Management makes estimates and assumptions affecting reported amounts. Significant items subject to estimates include useful lives of property and equipment and allowances for doubtful accounts.
Highly liquid instruments purchased with maturity of three months or less.

Cash related to development funds held in escrow per applicable state franchising laws, as well as cash related to requirements by the Partnership's financial institution. Approximately $431,000, $531,000, and $838,000 held as restricted cash at December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022 respectively. Classification based on expected date restrictions will be released.

Financial instruments subject to credit risk consist primarily of cash. Partnership maintains substantially all day-to-day cash balances with a major financial institution. At various times balances were in excess of FDIC insurance limits. Partnership monitors cash balances daily and maintains cash balances with high-credit-quality financial institutions.

As of January 1, 2023, adopted ASU 2016-13 (CECL model). Receivables include trade accounts receivable, contract assets, notes and loans receivable due from officers, owners, and employees. Assessed collectively based on age, nature, past due accounts, historical losses, existing economic conditions, and specific analysis. No current expected credit losses at December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022. Opening balance of accounts receivable as of January 1, 2022 approximately $375,000.

Carried at cost less accumulated depreciation. Straight-line method. Long-lived assets reviewed for impairment whenever events indicate carrying amount may not be recoverable. No impairment during years ended December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022. Estimated useful lives: property and equipment, computer hardware, software, and office equipment — three years.

Accounts for operating lease under ASU No. 2016-02 (Topic 842). Classification evaluation begins at commencement date; lease term includes noncancelable period plus periods where Partnership has right to use underlying asset, together with renewal option periods when exercise is reasonably certain. One lease classified as operating lease under ASC 842. No finance leases.

Represents right to use underlying asset for lease term. Lease liability represents obligation to make lease payments. Lease liability recognized at commencement based on present value of future lease payments discounted at risk-free rate (discount rate) corresponding to lease term.

notes.title: Contract Liabilities and Assets
notes.detail: Contract liability (deferred revenue) is a liability related to a revenue-producing activity for which revenue has not yet been recognized. Contract assets are unearned commissions paid to customers but not yet amortized.

notes.title: Revenue Recognition
notes.detail: Revenue under ASC Topic 606. Requires deferral of initial and renewal fees and recognition as revenue over the period in which the franchisee receives and consumes benefits from the Partnership's ongoing performance obligation to provide a franchise license. Pattern of recognition generally occurs on a straight-line basis over initial term of related franchise agreement.

terminated_development_agreements_income.note: When required number of restaurants are not opened during specified time, or area developer has been provided notice of default and it is unlikely that future development will occur, the development agreement may be terminated or modified. The deposit is recognized as income to the extent it is nonrefundable and the related area franchise rights could be resold.

notes.franchising_costs: Costs relating to franchise sales for which revenue has not been recognized are deferred and included as unearned commissions until related revenue is earned, unless related costs exceed anticipated revenue, in which case excess costs would be recorded as expense when incurred. When recognized, franchising costs are included in administrative and development costs.
notes.royalty_income: Based on fixed percentage of sales of franchised locations. Recorded sales-based royalty within provisions of ASC 606, as recorded as franchise revenue in statements of operations.
notes.other_revenue: Primarily consists of rebates received from equipment suppliers generally driven by volume and scope of purchases by franchisees. Rebates earned from suppliers approximately $980,000, $1,165,000, and $1,150,000 in 2024, 2023, and 2022 respectively. Supplier rebates received pursuant to binding agreements and based on specified cumulative level of purchases.

sub_notes.detail: Financial instruments included cash and cash equivalents, restricted cash, trade receivables, and accounts payable. Due to short-term maturities, carrying amounts approximate fair value.
Consist of costs associated with administrative support functions. Costs primarily consist of payroll, rent, travel, insurance, and professional expenses.

In accordance with federal income tax regulations, income taxes are the responsibility of the partners and are not levied on the Partnership. Accordingly, income taxes have not been reflected in the accompanying financial statements. Partnership accounts for uncertainty in tax positions; recognizes tax benefit from uncertain position only if it is at least more likely than not that the tax position will be sustained. No uncertain tax positions identified. No accruals have been made in financial statements.

Events or transactions that occur after balance sheet date but before financial statements are available to be issued. Partnership has evaluated subsequent events through February 28, 2025 and concluded no subsequent events have occurred that would require recognition or disclosure.

detail.general_partner: BPRGP — wholly owned subsidiary of Boston Pizza Restaurants (USA), Inc. (BPRUSA). Other entities owned by BPRUSA are considered related parties of the Partnership.
detail.restaurant_operations: Partnership occasionally pays for expenses related to operations of one restaurant location owned by a related party. For years ended December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022 expenses paid by the Partnership were approximately $559,000, $736,000, and $413,000 respectively. For year ended December 31, 2022, no amounts were paid for or received by the Partnership. As of December 31, 2024 and 2023 approximately $559,000 and $706,000 of these expenses were sitting in related party receivable/payable at financial position.
detail.management_services: Partnership has management services agreement with related-party franchisor of Boston's The Gourmet Pizza ® brand outside of US. Under agreement, Partnership earned service fees of approximately $265,000, $245,000, and $117,000 for years ended December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022 respectively. All management service fees recorded within other revenue in accompanying statements of operations. As of December 31, 2024, approximately $24,000 of this revenue was sitting in related party receivable.
detail.marketing_agreement: Partnership has management services agreement with a subsidiary of BPRUSA that performs certain marketing activities that promote Boston's The Gourmet Pizza ® brand. Under agreement, Partnership earned service fees of approximately $24,000, $24,900, and $120,600 for years ended December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022 respectively. All management service fees recorded within other revenue.

detail.description: In October 2020, Partnership amended its lease agreement for office space for support functions under a noncancelable operating lease expiring March 2029.
detail.note: Other components of lease costs were de minimis for years ended December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022.

notes.title: Commitments and Contingencies
notes.detail: Partnership subject to legal action and claims arising in ordinary course of business. Although outcome of any legal matter cannot be predicted with certainty, Partnership does not believe that any legal proceedings, matters in which Partnership is currently involved, will have material adverse effect on its business, financial position, results of operations, or cash flows.
